name: Harissa & Preserved Lemon Roasted Poussins
intro: Some of the best recipes in my repertoire were born out of convenience and this particular dish is one of those. It combines two North African cupboard staples, harissa (a popular chilli paste) and preserved lemons, to make a delicious marinade that is pretty hard to beat. Poussins are common in the Middle East, where they are revered for their tenderness and, even better, are ready in half the time it takes to cook a normal-sized chicken. So this is the perfect recipe for those nights when you come home late and want dinner within the hour. The results are fantastic and a whole poussin per person looks pretty spectacular.
method: Using a food processor or hand blender, blitz the preserved lemons, olive oil, salt, pepper to taste and rose harissa together until you achieve a smooth mixture. Pour this over your poussins and rub the mix into the birds using your hands.

Preheat the oven to 220°C/fan 200°C/gas mark 7. Place the poussins in a large roasting tray or ovenproof dish lined with nonstick baking paper. Roast the poussins for 45–50 minutes until cooked through and nicely browned on top – check the juices run clear when pierced at the thickest part of the thigh. Serve immediately.
